meta.title: Be Humble / Love Your Enemies
meta.verseText: Don't do anything only to get ahead. Don't do it because you are proud. Instead, be humble. Value others more than yourselves.
meta.theme: Upside-Down Love — Loving others like Jesus even when it's hard

questions.text: What Bible book is today's story from?
questions.hint: It's the first book of the New Testament.
questions.explanation: Today's lesson comes from Matthew 5:38-48, where Jesus teaches about loving your enemies.

questions.text: Where was Jesus when He taught the crowd about loving your enemies?
questions.hint: Think about going UP to talk to a big group.
questions.explanation: Jesus went up on a mountainside and a huge crowd gathered to listen to His teaching.

questions.text: What is today's Bottom Line?
options: Love like Jesus even when it's hard
Only love people who love you
questions.hint: It's about loving like someone special.
questions.explanation: The Bottom Line is 'Love like Jesus even when it's hard.' Jesus calls us to love everyone, not just the people who are easy to love.

questions.text: What does the word 'humble' mean in today's lesson?
questions.hint: It's the opposite of being proud.
questions.explanation: Being humble means putting others first, not trying to be the most important person in the room.

questions.text: Jesus taught us to love our enemies.
questions.explanation: Jesus clearly taught in Matthew 5 that we should love our enemies, not just the people who are nice to us.

questions.text: According to the memory verse, we should value _______ more than ourselves.
questions.hint: Think about the people around you.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 says to 'value others more than yourselves,' reminding us to put other people's needs and feelings before our own.

questions.text: What is the name of the series this lesson is part of?
questions.hint: It's the opposite of right side up!
questions.explanation: The series is called 'Upside Down' because Jesus teaches us to live in ways that are the opposite of what the world expects.

questions.text: Why does Jesus' teaching feel 'upside down'?
options: Because He stood on His head
Because it's the opposite of what the world usually does
Because He only talked to kids
questions.hint: Think about how the world usually acts versus how Jesus says to act.
questions.explanation: Jesus' teaching feels upside down because He asks us to do the opposite of what feels natural — like loving people who are mean to us instead of being mean back.

questions.text: We only need to be kind to people who are kind to us first.
questions.explanation: This is false. Jesus taught that real love means choosing kindness even when someone isn't kind to us first. That's what makes it upside-down love!

questions.text: The memory verse says: 'Don't do anything only to get _______.'
questions.hint: It's about trying to be in front of everyone else.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 starts by telling us not to do things just to get ahead of others — that's a selfish motivation.

questions.text: Who helps us love people who are hard to love?
questions.hint: We can't do it on our own — we need help from above!
questions.explanation: The lesson teaches that we can't love our enemies on our own. God and the Holy Spirit help us choose love and kindness when it's hard.

questions.text: What can you do when someone is hard to love?
options: Pause and talk to God about it
questions.hint: It involves talking to someone who always listens.
questions.explanation: When someone frustrates us, we can pause and pray. We can ask God to give us patience and help us respond with love instead of anger.

questions.text: What does it mean to 'value others more than yourselves'?
options: Let other people always win at games
Think you're not important at all
questions.hint: It's about how you treat the people around you.
questions.explanation: Valuing others more than yourself means treating people like they matter — showing them respect, kindness, and love, because they DO matter to God.

questions.text: When we choose to love someone who is hard to love, people will see that we follow Jesus.
questions.explanation: When people see us choosing love when it's hard, they can tell we're different. They see we're followers of Jesus because that kind of love is powerful and unusual.

questions.text: What is one way you can show upside-down love at school?
options: Only play with your best friends
Say something kind to someone who was mean to you
Try to be the most popular kid
questions.hint: Think about what Jesus would do.
questions.explanation: Saying something kind to someone who was mean to you is a perfect example of upside-down love — responding with kindness instead of meanness.

questions.text: The memory verse comes from the book of _______.
questions.hint: It starts with the letter P.
questions.explanation: The monthly memory verse is Philippians 2:3, written by the apostle Paul.

questions.text: What did Jesus' teaching sound like to the people listening?
options: Completely different from how they usually lived
Just like what their other teachers said
questions.hint: It was surprising to them!
questions.explanation: Jesus' teaching sounded completely different from the way people were used to living. He talked about humility, kindness, and loving difficult people — ideas that shocked the crowd.

questions.text: Loving your enemies is something you can easily do all by yourself.
questions.explanation: Loving our enemies is NOT easy and we can't do it alone. We need God's help! The Holy Spirit lives inside believers and gives us the strength to love when it's hard.

questions.text: Instead of being proud, the memory verse says to be _______.
questions.hint: It's the opposite of being full of yourself.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 says 'Instead, be humble.' Humble means putting others first and not thinking you're better than everyone else.

questions.text: If someone takes your spot in line, what would 'upside-down love' look like?
options: Push them out of the way
Let it go and choose to be kind
Tell the teacher so they get in trouble
questions.hint: Think about what Jesus would want you to do.
questions.explanation: Upside-down love means choosing kindness even when someone does something unfair. Letting it go and being kind is how we respond like Jesus.

questions.text: What came to live inside of us when we began following Jesus?
questions.hint: It's God's own Spirit.
questions.explanation: When we began following Jesus, the Holy Spirit came to live inside of us. The Holy Spirit helps us love others the way Jesus does.

questions.text: Which chapter and verses in Matthew does today's lesson come from?
questions.hint: It's in chapter 5.
questions.explanation: Today's Bible story comes from Matthew 5:38-48, part of Jesus' famous teaching on the mountainside.

questions.text: You should only pray for people who are nice to you.
options: True, mean people don't deserve prayer
questions.explanation: This is false! Jesus taught us to pray for people who upset us too. Praying for those who are hard to love is one way we can show upside-down love.

questions.text: What happens when we practice choosing kindness even when it feels awkward?
options: We grow stronger in our faith
questions.hint: Practice makes you better!
questions.explanation: Just like practicing anything that feels awkward at first makes you stronger, choosing kindness even when it's hard makes our faith grow stronger over time.

questions.text: When someone hurts your feelings, Jesus wants you to pour out _______ in response.
questions.hint: It's the most powerful thing you can give.
questions.explanation: Jesus wants us to pour out love in response to hurt. That's what upside-down love looks like — choosing love even when we've been hurt.

questions.text: Your friend said something mean about you. What should you do?
options: Pray for them and choose to respond with kindness
questions.hint: What would Jesus do?
questions.explanation: Praying for someone who hurts us and choosing kindness is exactly what Jesus taught. It's hard, but with God's help, we can show upside-down love.

questions.text: The lesson says choosing to love when it's hard is actually _______ to Jesus.
questions.hint: It means doing what Jesus asked.
questions.explanation: Choosing kindness when someone frustrates you is actually obedience to Jesus. He commands us to love our enemies, and when we do it, we're obeying Him.

questions.text: Jesus only taught us to love people who are easy to love.
questions.explanation: False! Jesus specifically taught us to love our ENEMIES — the people who are hardest to love. That's what makes His teaching so upside down!

questions.text: How can showing love to someone who is mean help them?
options: It won't help them at all
They might see Jesus through your kindness
It will make them feel guilty
It's not supposed to help them
questions.hint: Your love can point them to someone greater.
questions.explanation: When people see you choosing love when it's hard, they can see that you're different — a follower of Jesus. Your kindness might help them want to know Jesus too.

questions.text: What three things did Jesus talk about in His mountainside teaching?
questions.hint: They're all about how to treat people.
questions.explanation: On the mountainside, Jesus talked about humility, kindness, and loving others — even people who were difficult to love.

questions.text: According to the lesson, what can you ask God for when someone makes you mad?
options: To move to a new school
questions.hint: It's the opposite of anger.
questions.explanation: When someone makes us mad, we can ask God to give us patience instead of anger. God is always ready to help us respond with love.

questions.text: The verse says don't do anything because you are _______.
questions.hint: It's a feeling of thinking you're better than others.
questions.explanation: Philippians 2:3 warns us not to do things because we are proud. Pride makes us think we're better than others, but God wants us to be humble.

questions.text: Why is loving your enemies one of the most powerful ways to live like Jesus?
options: Because it's easy and everyone does it
Because it shows the world something totally different and points people to Jesus
Because enemies will always become your best friends
questions.hint: It shows the world something they don't usually see.
questions.explanation: Loving your enemies is powerful because it's so unusual — it shows the world that you're living differently, and it points people to Jesus and His love.

questions.text: A kid at recess won't let you play. What is an upside-down love response?
options: Go tell on them right away
Say 'That's okay' and ask someone else to play, then invite them again tomorrow
questions.hint: Respond with kindness, not anger.
questions.explanation: Showing grace and trying again later is upside-down love in action. You're choosing kindness instead of revenge, just like Jesus taught.

questions.text: God's love for us never gives up.
questions.explanation: The prayer in today's lesson thanks God for loving us with a love that never gives up. No matter what, God keeps loving us!

termPairs.definition: Putting others first instead of yourself
Living the opposite way the world expects — the way Jesus teaches
People who are not kind to us or who we don't get along with
God's Spirit who lives inside believers and helps them love others
The memory verse about being humble and valuing others
The Bible passage where Jesus teaches about loving your enemies
Thinking you are better than everyone else
What we can ask God for instead of anger
Choosing to do what Jesus asks even when it's hard
The place where Jesus taught a huge crowd about upside-down love
Love like Jesus even when it's hard

keyWords.hint: Putting others first; the opposite of proud
People who are not kind to us
What Jesus tells us to show even to difficult people
He taught on a mountainside about a new way to live
Choosing to be nice even when someone is mean
Thinking you are better than others; the memory verse warns against this
What to ask God for instead of anger
The Bible book where today's story is found
The Bible book where the memory verse is found
Doing what Jesus asks, even when it's not easy
Jesus went up on one of these to teach the crowd
God's Spirit who came to live inside believers
Letting go of anger toward someone who hurt you

story.summary: Jesus went up on a mountainside and taught a huge crowd of people a completely new way of living. Instead of getting revenge or only being kind to friends, Jesus said to love your enemies, choose kindness when people are mean, and be humble by putting others first. This 'upside-down' teaching shocked everyone because it was so different from how the world usually works. But Jesus promised that God's Holy Spirit would help us live this way — and when we do, people can see we are followers of Jesus.

scenes.title: Jesus Teaches on the Mountain
scenes.description: One day, Jesus went up on a mountainside and a huge crowd of people came to listen. What He said sounded completely different from the way people were used to living. He talked about humility, kindness, and loving others — even people who were difficult to love.

scenes.title: The Upside-Down Way to Live
scenes.description: Jesus taught that instead of getting even with people who are mean, we should choose love. Instead of only being kind to our friends, we should love our enemies too. This teaching flipped everything upside down! It was the total opposite of what people expected.

scenes.title: Be Humble and Value Others
scenes.description: The memory verse from Philippians 2:3 teaches the same upside-down idea: don't try to get ahead, don't be proud, be humble, and value others more than yourself. Being humble means putting others first and treating everyone like they matter.

scenes.title: We Need God's Help
scenes.description: Loving people who are hard to love isn't something we can do by ourselves. We need God's help! The Holy Spirit lives inside every believer and gives us the strength to be patient, kind, and loving. When we pause and pray, God gives us what we need to show upside-down love.

scenes.title: Living Upside-Down Love Every Day
scenes.description: When people see us choosing love even when it's hard, they know we're different — they can see we follow Jesus. We can say kind words instead of mean ones, pray for people who upset us, and forgive those who hurt our feelings. Every act of upside-down love makes us look more like Jesus.
